---
title: "Tenant Email Notification System"
subtitle: "Migrating from Manual Outlook Lists to a HubSpot CRM"
author: ""
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "On this page"
    smooth-scroll: true
    theme: flatly
    css: email-crm-project.css
    page-layout: full
---

# 

# 

```{=html}
<div class="hero-banner">
  <div class="hero-content">
    <h1 class="hero-title">Tenant Email Notification System</h1>
    <p class="hero-subtitle">
      Replacing a manual Outlook-based distribution list with a
      fully automated CRM pipeline powered by HubSpot 
    </p>
    </p>
    </p>
    <div class="hero-stats">
      <div class="stat-chip">📬 Marketing & CRM</div>
      <div class="stat-chip">✅ Self-updating contact lists</div>
      <div class="stat-chip">📊 Open &amp; click-rate analytics</div>
      <div class="stat-chip">🔒 One-click unsubscribe</div>
      <div class="stat-chip">📱 Mobile-friendly emails</div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```



## Project Overview

::: {.project-overview-intro}

Property Management Office previously relied on a fully manual email workflow: contacts were individually entered into Outlook folders based on tenant self-submitted forms, with no automated way to update, remove, or segment contacts. This project replaced that system end-to-end with a HubSpot CRM, delivering a dynamic, compliant, and analytics-ready email notification platform.

:::

## Tools & Technologies

| Tool                | Role                                              |
|---------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| **HubSpot**         | CRM, contact management, email builder, analytics |
| **Bitly**           | URL shortening for printed materials and QR codes |
| **Microsoft Excel** | Data export / migration from Outlook              |
| **Outlook**         | Legacy system (replaced)                          |

------------------------------------------------------------------------


```{=html}
<div class="card-row">
  <div class="info-card problem-card">
    <div class="card-icon">⚠️</div>
    <h3>The Problem</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Static contact lists required constant manual upkeep</li>
      <li>No automated bounce-back removal</li>
      <li>Opt-outs required emailing staff directly</li>
      <li>General and sensitive notifications shared the same list</li>
      <li>No open/click tracking or delivery analytics</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
  <div class="info-card solution-card">
    <div class="card-icon">✅</div>
    <h3>The Solution</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>HubSpot CRM with dynamic, self-updating contact lists</li>
      <li>Custom email sign-up form with automatic contact creation</li>
      <li>Resubmission auto-updates existing contacts</li>
      <li>Personalization tokens (e.g., tenant name in greeting)</li>
      <li>Built-in unsubscribe, mobile-optimized templates</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</div>
```


::: {.project-challenge-intro}

## The Challenge: A Broken Manual System

The legacy process for managing tenant communications was outdated and created significant operational overhead and compliance risk.

:::

**How it worked before:**

1.  A tenant submitted a paper or digital form with their contact information
2.  Staff manually typed each contact into a named Outlook folder (one per building)
3.  Contacts stayed in that folder indefinitely unless manually moved
4.  Bounce-backs required staff to notice and remove addresses by hand
5.  Opt-out requests arrived via individual emails to the office

This approach did not scale, produced stale lists, and offered no visibility into whether emails were actually being read.

## The Solution: HubSpot CRM Pipeline

```{=html}
<div class="pipeline-steps">
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-number">1</div>
    <div class="step-content">
      <h4>Data Migration</h4>
      <p>Existing Outlook contacts exported to <code>.xls</code> and bulk-imported into HubSpot, preserving all prior tenant records.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-arrow">→</div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-number">2</div>
    <div class="step-content">
      <h4>Sign-up Form</h4>
      <p>A HubSpot-hosted form published via QR code and short link (Bitly) captures new subscribers automatically.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-arrow">→</div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-number">3</div>
    <div class="step-content">
      <h4>Dynamic Lists</h4>
      <p>Contacts are searchable and filterable. Form submissions auto-create or auto-update records — no manual entry.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-arrow">→</div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-number">4</div>
    <div class="step-content">
      <h4>Email Campaigns</h4>
      <p>One-time blasts or automated sequences with personalization tokens, branded templates, and compliance-ready unsubscribe links.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-arrow">→</div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-number">5</div>
    <div class="step-content">
      <h4>Analytics</h4>
      <p>Delivery, open rate, and click-through rate tracked per campaign — closing the loop on what actually reaches tenants.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### Key Feature Highlights

**Dynamic Contact Management** HubSpot's list engine means contacts are always current. Resubmitting the sign-up form automatically updates an existing record rather than creating a duplicate — solving the stale-data problem that plagued Outlook folders.

**Compliant Opt-Out** Every email includes a one-click unsubscribe footer, meeting CAN-SPAM requirements without requiring tenants to contact the office.

**Personalization Tokens** Email greetings auto-populate with the tenant's name, making mass communications feel more personal and increasing engagement.

**Delivery Analytics** The HubSpot dashboard tracks delivery status, open rates, and click-through rates per campaign, giving the property management team concrete feedback on communication effectiveness.

**Campus-Wide Rollout** The launch used a printed flyer with QR code, a Bitly short link for digital sharing, and an introductory email to the existing list — all inviting tenants to opt in to the new system.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Outcomes & Impact

```{=html}
<div class="outcomes-grid">
  <div class="outcome-card">
    <div class="outcome-icon">⏱️</div>
    <h4>Time Saved</h4>
    <p>Eliminated manual contact entry and bounce-back monitoring — routine list maintenance now requires no staff intervention.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="outcome-card">
    <div class="outcome-icon">📈</div>
    <h4>Better Reach</h4>
    <p>The QR-code and short-link sign-up flow makes it easy to grow the list beyond the original tenant base to campus employees and visitors.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="outcome-card">
    <div class="outcome-icon">🔍</div>
    <h4>Visibility</h4>
    <p>For the first time, the team can see open rates, click rates, and delivery stats — enabling data-driven improvements to future campaigns.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="outcome-card">
    <div class="outcome-icon">⚖️</div>
    <h4>Compliance</h4>
    <p>Built-in unsubscribe links and opt-in forms align the system with email marketing best practices and CAN-SPAM requirements.</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------
